content:
- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md, resolved blockers against completed tasks, and confirmed there are no active bug tasks at all.
- Determined the unblocked failing candidates are ctl story tasks 08, 09, 10 and logging 01. Blocked items: cert-reload 01 blocked by ctl 08; config-simplification 01 blocked by full ctl story; general-architecture 01 and 02 blocked by ctl 10; postgres-role-reconciliation 01 blocked by config-simplification.
- Chose .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/10-task-collapse-dcs-behind-a-single-private-component-and-read-only-dcs-view.md as the next task and wrote it to .ralph/current_task.txt. Reason: among unblocked failing tasks, 08 and 10 are both high priority, but 10 is the stronger architectural unlock because it unblocks two medium-priority architecture tasks and removes a cross-cutting DCS boundary problem that other work will otherwise build on top of.

- Started this turn by reading .ralph/current_task.txt and locating the active task file.
- progress_read.sh did not return any prior progress content, so I am treating this as a fresh context window.
- Next I am reading the task body to determine whether it is still TO BE VERIFIED or already NOW EXECUTE.

- The task file has no TO BE VERIFIED/NOW EXECUTE marker, so per instructions this turn is the type-design pass.
- I am now auditing DCS types and external call sites before doing a broad boundary rewrite that may intentionally leave the tree uncompilable.
- Next I need the current runtime wiring, DCS module surface, and leaked type usage map.

- Read prior progress log; prior context says this turn is a DCS type-design audit.
- Initial path probe showed there is no top-level runtime/ha path; code appears under src/, so I need to resolve the real module locations before mapping consumers.
- Next I am searching for runtime/ha modules and DCS identifiers with ripgrep.

- Confirmed the current architecture leak: runtime creates three EtcdDcsStore instances, HA stores a DcsLeaderStore trait object, API stores a DcsStore trait object, and NodeState exposes full DcsState with cache internals.
- I am now reading the DCS worker and adapter to align the upcoming type rewrite with the intended single-owner component boundary.
- The design pass will rename/split public view types away from internal records and introduce a typed handle surface before any compile-fix work.

- Mapped direct DCS consumers across runtime, ha, api, and cli.
- Read dcs/state.rs plus runtime/node.rs, ha/worker.rs, ha/process_dispatch.rs, api/controller.rs, api/worker.rs, cli/status.rs, cli/connect.rs, and cli/switchover.rs.
- Next I am checking HA context/source helper files to capture any remaining leaked DCS types before writing the replacement surface summary.

- Inspected src/dcs/{mod,state,store,worker,etcd_store,keys}. The public data surface is mostly in dcs/state.rs; command/store concerns are split across dcs/store.rs and dcs/etcd_store.rs.
- Traced external coupling: runtime/node wires DcsState channels plus concrete EtcdDcsStore instances; ha/state and ha/worker consume DcsState plus DcsLeaderStore; api/worker and api/controller consume DcsState plus raw DcsStore; CLI and API NodeState expose raw DcsState/MemberSlot shapes.
- Minimal boundary that fits current code: keep existing member/lease/switchover record types as payloads, add a read-only DcsView replacing broad DcsState exposure, and a scope/self-bound DcsCommandHandle that covers acquire/release lease plus request/clear switchover. First signature changes should start at dcs/state.rs + dcs/store.rs, then runtime/node wiring, then ha/api context and controller entrypoints.

- Scanned tutorial/reference docs and reconciled them against current repository state.
- Found major drift: tutorials reference missing files/commands (`docker/compose/docker-compose.single.yml`, `.env.docker.example`, `tools/docker/cluster.sh`) and non-existent `pgtm debug` subcommand.
- Verified CLI reality from code:
  - `pgtm` supports `status`, `primary`, `replicas`, `switchover request|clear`.
  - `status` has `--watch/--json/-v`; no debug command exists.
- Verified docker stack reality:
  - `docker-compose.yml` exists and includes etcd + node-a/b/c services.
  - Node API defaults to HTTPS on 8443 in container and PostgreSQL host/port mapping to 15001/15002/15003 via compose.
- Next: write full rewritten tutorial drafts in `.ralph/archive/evidence/docs/` so they reflect actual commands and avoid unnecessary info overload.

- Wrote full tutorial rewrite drafts under `.ralph/archive/evidence/docs/tutorial/` and added path-level `.gitignore` files so these files stay isolated.
- Drafts now use only active project surfaces: `docker compose` commands, `docker/pgtm.toml`, and `pgtm status/primary/replicas`.
- Removed references to stale paths/commands in these tutorial drafts (`tools/docker/cluster.sh`, `docker-compose.single.yml`, `pgtm debug`, missing `.env.docker.example`, TLS mismatched sample configs).
- Next action: review content with you and adjust based on whether you want to keep single-node in this path as a temporary stack or make it optional.

- Removed non-functional `echo` placeholder in archived first-HA tutorial.
- Replaced it with real Docker-based endpoint checks: `docker compose port node-a 5432`, `node-b`, `node-c`, and `docker compose ps`.
- This keeps the tutorial instructional surface tied to verifiable commands only.

- Adjusted tutorial guidance after your note:
  - kept `docker compose ps` as the preferred first check for running services and ports.
  - moved explicit host->container port lookup to a secondary step via `docker compose port`.
  - added in-container `pgtm` workflow using official compose exec pattern:
    `docker compose exec node-a pgtm -c /etc/pgtuskmaster/runtime.toml status`.
- Added this option to tutorial overview and first-ha tutorial in archived evidence docs.

- Removed the JSON-to-file redirection from archived first-HA tutorial.
- Added representative sample outputs for:
  - `docker compose ps` (service state + exposed host ports)
  - `pgtm -c docker/pgtm.toml status -v` (human status table)
- Replaced that section with optional raw state note as an inline `status --json` command only.

- Added a new public DCS boundary sketch in code: DcsView for read access and DcsHandle for typed commands, with internal MemberRecord/SwitchoverRecord/cache types split away from the public view.
- I am now sweeping remaining old DCS type/store references so the repo is consistently in the new type world before stopping.
- This is intentionally a design-only pass; I am not fixing compile errors or validation failures in this turn.

- Completed the design-only pass required before execution. I introduced a new public DCS boundary in code: DcsView for read-only state and DcsHandle plus DcsCommand for typed mutations.
- Split the existing storage-shaped DCS types into internal *Record/*Cache types versus public *View types, and pushed those new type names through DCS, API, HA, CLI, and runtime signatures in one broad sweep.
- Updated the active task file to <status>in_progress</status> and appended NOW EXECUTE at the end. I intentionally did not fix compiler errors, run make targets, or repair the runtime wiring fully in this turn because the instructions explicitly required quitting after the type-design phase.
- The next turn should start from NOW EXECUTE, finish the compile-fix/integration work, then run make check, make lint, make test, make test-long, and only after that do docs updates if needed.

- Read the Ralph progress log and confirmed the previous turn already changed the active task into a NOW EXECUTE phase for the DCS boundary rewrite.
- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and identified the active task file under story-ctl-operator-experience.
- Next I am reading the task body and inspecting the worktree so I can execute the design, fix compile errors, and run the required make targets.

- Read the active task body; it is explicitly in NOW EXECUTE state, so this turn must finish the integration and validation work rather than another design-only pass.
- Checked git status and confirmed the prior turn already changed many DCS-facing files and added src/dcs/command.rs.
- Next I am baselining with make check and repo-wide grep verification to identify the remaining compile failures and architectural leaks to clean up.

- Wired the DCS worker so runtime now bootstraps through a single DCS-owned builder instead of constructing cache/store state directly in runtime.
- Folded leader-lease methods into the internal DcsStore trait, added DCS command processing in the worker, removed the stale leader deletion path, and fixed the API router to await asynchronously.
- Next I am using a fresh make check run to identify the remaining compile fallout, then I will sweep tests and finally remove the dead disabled runtime block cleanly.

- make check and make lint are now clean after shrinking the DCS internal surface further and moving external tests onto the public DcsView/DcsHandle boundary.
- I also removed the dead disabled runtime DCS test block by rewriting src/runtime/node.rs down to only the live code path.
- Next I am running make test and then make test-long before the docs update pass and final task bookkeeping.

- The ultra-long regression turned out to be a real HA bug: reconcile_follow_role treated Replica { upstream: None } as stable, so a detached/misconfigured replica could rejoin without ever being reconfigured to follow the authoritative leader.
- I changed that path to demote/restart the replica and added a focused reconcile unit test. The previously failing ultra-long scenario now passes when run in isolation.
- Next I am rerunning make check and then the full make test-long suite to get the required all-green evidence before docs and task finalization.

- Resumed the active DCS-boundary task and re-read the current task file plus the progress log.
- Confirmed the tree is in NOW EXECUTE state and that the remaining blockers are the ultra-long HA regressions after the refactor: switchover target eligibility, stopped-replica visibility, and restart convergence.
- Next I am reproducing those failures in a narrowed test set and tracing the DcsView-to-HA/API behavior differences before making more edits.

- Investigated the DCS privacy question without touching project code.
- Confirmed the big grep count is misleading: most pub(crate) items are inside private dcs submodules and are only shared across src/dcs siblings, not consumed by ha/api/cli.
- Found the remaining real crate-visible leak is runtime/node bootstrapping through crate::dcs::worker::{DcsWorkerBootstrap, build_worker_ctx, run}; store/etcd_store stay pub(crate) in mod.rs even though I found no non-dcs usages.

- Inspected the actual long-test run artifacts. The final-node-rejoin regression is concrete: after full outage the restarted node enters StartDetachedStandby, then remains Replica { upstream: None } with no follow source and never catches new writes.
- I also confirmed the replica-outage failure is mostly a test-surface mismatch: the helper counted raw DCS member slots, which still include the killed replica until its TTL expires, even though the step name is about remaining online nodes.
- Next I am patching three things together: generic planned switchover should publish AnyHealthyReplica, follower reconcile should demote/restart any detached replica with upstream None when StartStreaming is required, and the online-replica helper should filter to intended-online members.

- Updated the active DCS task file per user request, without touching source code.
- Appended acceptance criteria that require narrowing any unnecessary pub/pub(crate) visibility in src/dcs and treating leftover wider visibility as unfinished work.
- Also added a repo-wide lint/enforcement requirement so unnecessary exposed functions/structs/enums/modules/traits/etc are not allowed to accumulate again.

- The remaining ultra-long failures are now down: the two runtime/HA regressions were fixed in reconcile/decide, and the last stubborn concurrent planned switchover case is green after tightening the HA test helper to wait for and target the best currently eligible replica.
- make check is still clean after the latest patches, and the previously failing focused ultra-long cases now pass in isolation.
- Next I am running the required full validation matrix again: make lint, make test, and then full make test-long before deciding whether docs need updates and doing task-finalization bookkeeping.

- Resumed from context handoff with code and tests already green before docs edits.
- Next step is re-running validation after the doc updates, starting with make lint because it exercises docs checks too.

- make lint is running after docs edits.
- Waiting for the Rust and docs lint stack to finish before finalizing the task file.

- make lint is still compiling dependencies during the validation sweep.
- Holding off on task completion metadata until the post-docs validation actually finishes green.

- make lint progressed past cargo check and into the clippy phases.
- Finalization is still blocked only on the last validation command finishing successfully.

- make lint passed after the docs updates.
- Reading the task file to update its checklist state and flip passes to true before task switch and commit.

- Running final grep-based boundary audits before checking off the task acceptance criteria.
- Verifying the remaining etcd bootstrap site, absence of raw mutation calls outside DCS, and absence of leaked internal DCS imports.

- Tightened the grep audit and confirmed there are no low-level DCS mutation calls, leaked internal DCS imports, or extra EtcdDcsStore bootstrap sites outside src/dcs.
- Next step is updating the task file acceptance checklist and passes marker, then switching the task and committing.

- Updated the task file to completed with passes=true and checked acceptance criteria.
- Running task_switch.sh now so Ralph metadata advances before the final commit.
